title: Audio, video, tracks and embeds

publication.changeSummary: Technology-specific HTML: Semantic and Accessible Web Documents draft for Audio, video, tracks and embeds; independent standards, browser, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

sources.locator: HTML Living Standard; living standard accessed 2026-07; provisions applicable to Audio, video, tracks and embeds
sources.claim: Native audio and video controls provide playback semantics, while captions, transcripts, and audio description address different access needs.

sources.claim: Native audio and video controls provide playback semantics, while captions, transcripts, and audio description address different access needs.

canonicalExplanation.summary: Native audio and video controls provide playback semantics, while captions, transcripts, and audio description address different access needs.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A lesson video includes native controls, synchronized captions, and a linked transcript. Your goal is to embed accessible time-based media and explain cross-origin or third-party privacy concerns. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Relative and absolute URLs; Paragraph and inline meaning; Images and alternative text; External navigation safety. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Technology rule
sections.body: Native audio and video controls provide playback semantics, while captions, transcripts, and audio description address different access needs. Apply this rule to the actual markup, stylesheet, JavaScript, or component state rather than memorizing its name.

sections.heading: Boundary and browser reality
sections.body: Autoplay can be blocked or harmful, captions are not the same as transcripts, and custom controls need complete keyboard and name/state support. This draft therefore rejects: Adding controls makes spoken video content accessible without captions or transcript.

includes: Provide controls, captions or transcripts and meaningful fallback while avoiding unsolicited playback.
Embed accessible time-based media and explain cross-origin or third-party privacy concerns.
excludes: Autoplay can be blocked or harmful, captions are not the same as transcripts, and custom controls need complete keyboard and name/state support.

outcomes: Embed accessible time-based media and explain cross-origin or third-party privacy concerns.

examples.title: Audio, video, tracks and embeds: concrete case
examples.situation: A lesson video includes native controls, synchronized captions, and a linked transcript.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the exact artifact and starting state: A lesson video includes native controls, synchronized captions, and a linked transcript.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the correction: Provide controls plus synchronized captions for speech and a transcript or other alternatives appropriate to the media.
examples.result: The result follows because Provide controls plus synchronized captions for speech and a transcript or other alternatives appropriate to the media.

examples.title: Audio, video, tracks and embeds: boundary case
examples.situation: Autoplay can be blocked or harmful, captions are not the same as transcripts, and custom controls need complete keyboard and name/state support.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Start from the documented boundary: Autoplay can be blocked or harmful, captions are not the same as transcripts, and custom controls need complete keyboard and name/state support.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Reject the misconception: Adding controls makes spoken video content accessible without captions or transcript.
examples.result: The boundary requires this correction: Provide controls plus synchronized captions for speech and a transcript or other alternatives appropriate to the media.
